description: The byte-identical write from `write-not-in-logic-context-pos`, wrapped in an explicit `${ ... }` logic block — does NOT fire E-WRITE-NOT-IN-LOGIC-CONTEXT. The `${}` wrapper is the ONLY delta between the two cases, so the pair brackets the write/logic-context boundary exactly.
rationale: §40.8 S123 amendment names this as fix (a) verbatim: "The fix is either (a) wrap in an explicit logic block `${ @name = ... }`, or (b) convert to a structural declaration `<name> = ...`." Writes ARE logic; inside a logic block a write is in its proper context, so the code SHALL stay silent. The §34 row's discrimination note is what this NEG pins: the diagnostic fires at the IMMEDIATE body-top of the synthetic default-logic auto-lift wrapper ONLY — a write inside a user-written `${...}` block is out of Unit CC's fire region.
